beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make pour-over coffee at home in Ballard.
beats.reasoning: Nora wakes early as is her habit; she refuses drip coffee so makes pour-over at home. Yesterday's energy was moderate (40/100), so she eases into the day without rushing.

beats.activity: Bike commute to First Hill in light rain.
beats.reasoning: Steady rain from 7am to 2pm; Nora cycles regardless of drizzle and is prepared for wet weather. SR-520 closure doesn't affect her Ballard-to-First Hill route. Budget 45 min for rain-cautious cycling.

beats.activity: Work at transit agency: team standup, design review, stakeholder call.
beats.reasoning: Full morning of work meetings; yesterday ran long, so today Nora settles into steady work rhythm. Rain continues outside, making indoor focus easier.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and espresso at a nearby café.
beats.reasoning: Nora knows Vivace from yesterday; she avoids drip coffee and prefers quality espresso. Still raining, so staying local makes sense. Quick 35-min break.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: project documentation, email catch-up, design critiques.
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; Nora stays focused at the office. No major events today, so a solid afternoon of work. Yesterday's guilt about the long break makes her lean into productivity.
Weather is clearing by late afternoon. Nora heads to Capitol Hill to meet a close friend for dinner rather than biking straight home. 45 min allows for the cross-town ride and settling in.

beats.activity: Dinner with close friend at a cozy Capitol Hill restaurant.
beats.reasoning: Nora values time with close friends over big crowds. Yesterday was high-energy and social (Kremwerk), so tonight is intimate and intentional. She's socially engaged but not exhausted.
Evening is clear and mild after rain. Nora bikes home through quieter streets, enjoying the post-dinner air. 45 min for the cross-town ride back to Ballard.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: review film negatives, edit photos, light reading.
beats.reasoning: Nora winds down with her photography hobby. Yesterday she edited on laptop; today she reviews negatives. Gentle evening after a balanced day of work and social time.

Nora goes to bed at a reasonable hour after a balanced Tuesday. 8-hour sleep ends at 6:30am Wednesday, setting up an early start if desired. Yesterday's moderate energy (40/100) recovers with good rest.

diary: Woke to that familiar Seattle drizzle and decided the rain was no excuse to skip the bike ride—honestly, it cleared my head before work even started. The office was chaos this morning; SR-520 eastbound was a nightmare, so half the team was late, which somehow made our standup feel more urgent than usual. We're deep in the design review for the new light rail connector, and I caught myself getting defensive about a detail I'd pushed for. Grabbed a sandwich at Vivace around eleven and just sat there for a moment, watching the rain streak the windows. The afternoon felt lighter somehow. By the time I biked over to Capitol Hill to meet Sarah at Altura, the clouds had finally broken. We talked about everything—her new job, my frustration with the project, that old familiar ease between us. Rode home through damp streets with my jacket soaked but feeling oddly settled.
thought_process: The weather really got in my head this morning—usually I'd drive on a day like this, but something about the rain made me want to push through it on the bike. Work stress was high because of the bridge closure affecting our broader planning; it reminded me how fragile these systems are, which both energized and unsettled me. I think I was defensive in the design review because I've been carrying doubt about my own choices, and Sarah's presence tonight helped me let that go. The shift from morning tension to evening calm felt earned, not accidental.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
